source.title: "Don't Hire Humans" — A Catholic Response to AI | Catholic Futurist

summary.title_hr: Katolički odgovor na umjetnu inteligenciju: 'Ne zapošljavajte ljude'
summary.abstract_hr: Podcast 'Catholic Futurist' istražuje izazove i prilike koje umjetna inteligencija donosi obrazovanju i društvu, potaknut provokativnom porukom 'Ne zapošljavajte ljude. Zapošljavajte nas, robote.' Dr. Aaron Dominguez i John Brahier raspravljaju o tome kako katoličko obrazovanje, utemeljeno na razumijevanju ljudskog dostojanstva (Imago Dei), može pružiti etički okvir za razvoj i primjenu AI-ja. Naglašavaju važnost inkarnacijskog susreta u učionici i predstavljaju tri stupa za osiguravanje da tehnologija vodi ljudskom procvatu, a ne digitalnom redukcionizmu. Razgovor se dotiče i egzistencijalnih pitanja gubitka posla te potrebe za redefiniranjem svrhe i slobodnog vremena u svijetu kojim dominira AI.
key_topics: uloga Crkve u raspravi o AI-ju
key_points: Umjetna inteligencija (AI) već godinama utječe na učionice, a s pojavom alata poput ChatGPT-a, postala je neizbježna stvarnost koja prisiljava na preispitivanje filozofije obrazovanja i pedagogije.

Katoličko obrazovanje jedinstveno je opremljeno za vođenje u doba AI-ja jer nudi duboko razumijevanje ljudskog bića stvorenog na sliku Božju (Imago Dei), što pomaže u razlikovanju simulacije od stvarne osobe.
Dr. Dominguez predlaže tri stupa za interakciju čovjeka i AI-ja: čovjek kao glavni protagonist, AI kao alat za ljudski procvat i rast u vrlinama, te poticanje autentične ljudske interakcije.
Postoji zabrinutost da bi se ljudi mogli početi zrcaliti rezultatima LLM-ova i zvučati više poput njih, umjesto obrnuto, što zahtijeva primjenu dvotisućljetne mudrosti Crkve.
Gubitak posla zbog automatizacije postavlja egzistencijalna pitanja o ljudskom identitetu i svrsi, naglašavajući potrebu za pastoralnim odgovorom Crkve na izazove koje AI donosi.
Katoličke institucije mogu integrirati AI kroz edukaciju (npr. AI boot camp za biskupe), razvoj kurikuluma koji spaja tehnologiju s etikom i teologijom, te poticanjem dijaloga o ovim pitanjima.
summary.title_en: Catholic response to artificial intelligence: 'Don't hire people'
summary.abstract_en: The podcast 'Catholic Futurist' explores the challenges and opportunities that artificial intelligence brings to education and society, prompted by the provocative message 'Don't hire people. Hire us, robots.' Dr. Aaron Dominguez and John Brahier discuss how Catholic education, grounded in the understanding of human dignity (Imago Dei), can provide an ethical framework for the development and application of AI. They emphasize the importance of the incarnational encounter in the classroom and present three pillars for ensuring that technology leads to human flourishing, rather than digital reductionism. The conversation also touches on existential questions of job loss and the need for redefining purpose and leisure in a world dominated by AI.
key_topics_en: the future of work and AI
pedagogy in the age of AI
the role of the Church in the discussion about AI
key_points_en: Artificial intelligence (AI) has been affecting classrooms for years, and with the appearance of tools like ChatGPT, it has become an unavoidable reality that forces a rethinking of the philosophy of education and pedagogy.

Catholic education is uniquely equipped to lead in the age of AI because it offers a deep understanding of the human being created in the image of God (Imago Dei), which helps in distinguishing simulation from a real person.

Dr. Dominguez proposes three pillars for the interaction of man and AI: man as the main protagonist, AI as a tool for human flourishing and growth in virtues, and the fostering of authentic human interaction.

There is concern that people could begin to mirror themselves with the results of LLMs and sound more like them, instead of the other way around, which requires the application of the two-thousand-year-old wisdom of the Church.
Job loss due to automation poses existential questions about human identity and purpose, emphasizing the need for a pastoral response of the Church to the challenges that AI brings.

Catholic institutions can integrate AI through education (e.g., AI boot camp for bishops), development of curricula that connects technology with ethics and theology, and by encouraging dialogue about these issues.
